seoTitle: How to Link a Task to a Story in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to link a task to a story in Jira so related work stays connected and easy to trace back to its context.
intro: Not every relationship between issues needs a full parent-child hierarchy — sometimes you just need to show that a task relates to a specific story. This guide covers how to create that link inside Jira.

steps.heading: Open the task you want to link
steps.body: Go to the task's detail view where issue links can be added.

steps.heading: Find the link issue option
steps.body: Look for the link icon or menu option that lets you connect this issue to another.

steps.heading: Choose the link type
steps.body: Select a link type such as 'relates to' or 'is part of' that best describes the connection to the story.

steps.heading: Search for and select the story
steps.body: Type the story's key or title to find it, then confirm the link.

steps.heading: Verify the link on both issues
steps.body: Check that the link now appears on both the task and the story so the relationship is visible from either side.

faq.question: What link type should I use to connect a task to a story?
faq.answer: Most teams use a general 'relates to' link, though Jira offers other types like 'blocks' or 'is caused by' depending on the relationship.

faq.question: Is linking the same as making the task a subtask of the story?
faq.answer: No, linking keeps both issues independent, while making it a subtask creates a formal parent-child relationship.

faq.question: Can I link a task to multiple stories?
faq.answer: Yes, an issue can have multiple links to different stories or other issues at the same time.

faq.question: Do linked issues show up in reports together?
faq.answer: Some reports do reflect issue links, but this depends on the specific report and how your project is configured.
